questions.answer: The League of Nations was an international organisation formed after World War I with the aim of promoting peace and security. It was established by the Treaty of Versailles in 1919.

questions.q: What was the main objective of the United Nations Charter?
questions.answer: The main objective of the United Nations Charter is to save succeeding generations from the scourge of war and to promote international cooperation for peace and security.
questions.topic: Structure and Functions of the United Nations

questions.q: Mention one function of the International Labour Organisation (ILO).
questions.answer: One function of the ILO is to promote social and economic development by setting minimum standards for labour rights and working conditions.
questions.topic: Specialised Agencies of the United Nations

questions.q: Explain the role of the General Assembly in the United Nations.
questions.answer: The General Assembly plays a crucial role in the United Nations as it is the supreme deliberative and representative organ of the United Nations. It is composed of representatives from all member states and is responsible for making key decisions on global issues, discussing and making recommendations on the items on the agenda, and providing a forum for dialogue and cooperation among member states.
questions.topic: Structure and Functions of the United Nations

questions.q: Describe the functions of the International Court of Justice (ICJ).
questions.answer: The ICJ is the principal judicial organ of the United Nations and is responsible for settling disputes between states in accordance with international law. It also provides advisory opinions on international law issues and is the final arbiter in cases of disputes between states.
questions.topic: Structure and Functions of the United Nations

questions.q: What are the three main purposes of the United Nations?
questions.answer: The three main purposes of the United Nations are to maintain international peace and security, to develop friendly relations among nations based on respect for the principle of equal rights and self-determination of peoples, and to achieve international cooperation in solving international problems of an economic, social, cultural, or humanitarian character.
questions.topic: Purposes and Principles of the United Nations

questions.q: Mention one advantage and one disadvantage of international organisations.
questions.answer: One advantage of international organisations is that they promote international cooperation and provide a framework for addressing global issues. One disadvantage is that they may be ineffective in enforcing their decisions and may be subject to the veto power of powerful member states.
questions.topic: Role and Importance of International Organisations

questions.q: Explain the concept of collective security.
questions.answer: Collective security is a concept in which all member states of an international organisation agree to work together to maintain peace and security in the face of aggression or threats. It is based on the idea that the security of one state is linked to the security of all states.

questions.q: Describe the role of the United Nations in promoting disarmament.
questions.answer: The United Nations has played a crucial role in promoting disarmament by providing a framework for negotiating and implementing arms control agreements. It has also established various bodies and committees to monitor and verify disarmament efforts, and to provide technical assistance to states in disarmament-related activities.

questions.q: Explain the concept of international law and its importance.
questions.answer: International law is a set of rules and norms that govern the relationships between states and other international actors. It is based on the principle of sovereignty and equality among states, and is aimed at promoting peace, security, and cooperation among nations. International law is important because it provides a framework for resolving disputes and promoting cooperation among states, and it helps to maintain stability and predictability in international relations.

questions.q: Describe the role of the United Nations in promoting sustainable development.
questions.answer: The United Nations has played a crucial role in promoting sustainable development by providing a framework for implementing the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs). It has also established various bodies and mechanisms to support sustainable development efforts, including the High-Level Political Forum on Sustainable Development and the Committee of Experts on Public Administration.

questions.q: Explain the concept of non-state actors in international relations.
questions.answer: Non-state actors in international relations refer to individuals, groups, or organisations that are not part of the government or state, but that play a significant role in shaping international relations. Examples of non-state actors include non-governmental organisations (NGOs), multinational corporations, and transnational networks.

questions.q: Describe the role of the International Monetary Fund (IMF) in promoting global economic stability.
questions.answer: The IMF plays a crucial role in promoting global economic stability by providing financial assistance to countries in need, monitoring and advising on economic policies, and promoting international cooperation on economic issues. It has also established various mechanisms to support economic development and stability, including the Poverty Reduction and Growth Facility (PRGF) and the Stand-By Arrangement (SBA).
questions.topic: Specialised Agencies of the United Nations

questions.q: Explain the concept of human rights and their importance in international relations.
questions.answer: Human rights refer to the basic rights and freedoms that are inherent to all human beings, regardless of their nationality, place of residence, sex, national or ethnic origin, colour, religion, language, or any other status. Human rights are essential for promoting dignity, equality, and justice in international relations, and are protected by international law, including the Universal Declaration of Human Rights (UDHR) and the International Covenant on Civil and Political Rights (ICCPR).

questions.q: Describe the role of the World Health Organisation (WHO) in promoting global health.
questions.answer: The WHO plays a crucial role in promoting global health by providing technical assistance to countries, setting standards for health care, and promoting international cooperation on health issues. It has also established various mechanisms to support health development and stability, including the Global Health Observatory (GHO) and the World Health Assembly (WHA).
questions.topic: Specialised Agencies of the United Nations
